content.problemStatement:
Chef has three socks in his drawer. Each sock has one of 101010 possible colours, which are represented by integers between 111 and 101010. Specifically, the colours of the socks are AAA, BBB, and CCC.

Chef has to wear two socks which have the same colour. Help Chef find out if that is possible or not.


Input Format
The first and only line of the input contains three space-separated integers AAA, BBB and CCC.



Output Format
Print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible for Chef to wear two socks with the same colour or `"NO"` if it is impossible (without quotes).
You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C denoting the colours of the three socks.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing "YES" if at least two of the three integers are equal, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: All three colours are distinct, so Chef cannot pick two socks of the same colour.
All three socks share the same colour, therefore a matching pair exists.
First test case: colours 1 and 1 match → YES. Second test case: all colours are different → NO.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to know whether any two of the three numbers are the same. The simplest way is to compare the three possible pairs: (A==B) || (A==C) || (B==C). If any comparison is true we print YES, otherwise NO. This runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        a = int(next(it)); b = int(next(it)); c = int(next(it))
        out.append('YES' if a==b or a==c or b==c else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
